explanation: We interpret this as: Whenever prop_0 holds, then prop_1 must hold either (i) after prop_2 has occurred sometime in the future, or (ii) as soon as prop_3 is true. So, for all times where prop_0 holds, at least one of these is eventually satisfied. In LTL, this becomes: G ( prop_0 -> ( F(prop_2) -> F(prop_1) ) | ( F(prop_3 -> prop_1) ) ).

This statement says: 'When prop_0, prop_1 either after prop_2, or when prop_3.' This means: If prop_0 is true, then (eventually after prop_2, prop_1 holds), or (when prop_3 holds, prop_1 holds). In LTL, this is encoded as: if prop_0 then (either eventually (prop_2 and after that, eventually prop_1), or (whenever prop_3, prop_1)). We use (prop_2 -> F prop_1) to mean 'after prop_2, eventually prop_1,' and (prop_3 -> prop_1) for 'when prop_3, prop_1.' These two are combined with OR, and the implication is over prop_0.

The requirement says: 'When prop_0, prop_1 either after prop_2, or when prop_3.' This specifies that whenever prop_0 holds, 'prop_1' must happen either after 'prop_2' has happened (i.e., prop_1 Until prop_2) or at every point that prop_0 holds, prop_1 must hold if prop_3 holds. Expressed in LTL: whenever prop_0, then either eventually after prop_2, prop_1 holds, or while prop_0 holds, prop_3 implies prop_1.

The requirement says: Whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 should hold either after prop_2 happens (i.e., eventually-after prop_2) or whenever prop_3 holds (i.e., globally with prop_3, prop_1 must also hold). In LTL: For all times, if prop_0 holds, then either eventually after prop_2, prop_1 holds (i.e., if prop_2 is ever true, eventually prop_1 becomes true after that), or, at any moment when prop_3 is true, prop_1 is also true.

The statement says: whenever 'prop_0' holds, then 'prop_1' should become true either after 'prop_2' holds or when 'prop_3' holds. We interpret this as: globally, if 'prop_0' is true, then eventually ('prop_2' U 'prop_1') or ('prop_3' -> 'prop_1') should be satisfied. However, since 'when prop_3' is used, we take this as: as soon as 'prop_3' becomes true, then 'prop_1' should also be true at that moment. The 'after prop_2' means: after 'prop_2' occurs (from that point), eventually 'prop_1' holds. Combined, this becomes: G(prop_0 -> ((F(prop_2) -> F(prop_1)) | G(prop_3 -> prop_1))). But for a stricter LTL translation to not use implications with F, we can phrase as: G(prop_0 -> (( (not prop_2) U (prop_2 & F prop_1) ) | G(prop_3 -> prop_1))).

Whenever prop_0 holds, a new inspiration (represented by some event, say 'insp') must be initiated. This initiation should occur either after prop_1 holds (i.e., eventually after prop_1) or when prop_2 holds (i.e., if prop_2 becomes true, inspiration is triggered). Since the event of initiating an inspiration isn't named as an atomic proposition, it's implied that 'inspiration is initiated' happens as soon as prop_1 or prop_2 holds after prop_0. LTL cannot reference events, only state, so we interpret that when prop_0 holds, eventually (prop_1 or prop_2) will be true, at which point a new inspiration starts.

When prop_0 is true, then prop_1 must either occur strictly after prop_2, or (alternatively) when prop_3 is true. This means that whenever prop_0 happens, eventually prop_1 happens, and when it does, it must be the case that previously prop_2 occurred, or at that moment prop_3 is true. So: globally, if prop_0 occurs, then eventually prop_1 holds, and when prop_1 holds, either prop_2 has occurred before, or prop_3 is true at that time.

This formula expresses that whenever prop_0 holds, then prop_1 must hold either after prop_2 happens (i.e., sometime after prop_2 becomes true) or when prop_3 holds. That is, after prop_0, prop_1 is required, and the requirement is satisfied if either prop_1 holds following prop_2, or whenever prop_3 is true, prop_1 is true.

The requirement states: When prop_0 holds, prop_3 must hold either after prop_1, or whenever prop_2 holds. This means: If prop_0 holds, then (1) if prop_1 happens at some point, afterwards prop_3 must be true from then on, OR (2) whenever prop_2 is true, prop_3 must also be true. This can be expressed as: Globally, if prop_0, then ( (if eventually prop_1, then after that, always prop_3) OR (always, if prop_2, then prop_3)).

The requirement describes a mode (PCVMode) where, under the condition 'prop_0', 'prop_1' must hold, but its enforcement is either after 'prop_2' has occurred or when 'prop_3' is true. Thus, if 'prop_0' holds, then 'prop_1' must hold globally after 'prop_2' occurs, or whenever 'prop_3' becomes true. This can be written as: if 'prop_0' holds, then ('prop_2' has occurred and from then on 'prop_1' holds globally) or ('prop_3' holds and so does 'prop_1'). Formally: G(prop_0 -> ((F prop_2 & G(prop_2 -> G(prop_1))) | (prop_3 & prop_1)))
